original_title: ASC23043 - Positive Behaviour Support Supported Living Services in Herefordshire & Worcestershire DPS
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for Positive Behaviour Support Supported Living Services in Herefordshire and Worcestershire, jointly established by Worcestershire County Council and NHS Worcestershire and Herefordshire Integrated Care Board. Suppliers can apply at any time during the DPS term, with evaluations conducted in periodic rounds, to pre-qualify for future mini competitions and individual placement referrals.
what_they_want: NOT FOUND
eligibility_requirements: Suitably experienced, qualified and appropriately accredited suppliers who can meet selection criteria and minimum standards as requested in the SSQ Questionnaire (Stage 1) and Evaluation (Stage 2). No maximum number of suppliers.
evaluation_criteria: 3-stage process: Stage 1 - SSQ Questionnaire (selection criteria and minimum standards), Stage 2 - Evaluation, Stage 3 - Mini Competitions for scheme/cluster placements or Referrals for individual placements. Specific weighting criteria not mentioned.

original_title: Contract Notice
english_summary: UK Health Security Agency is establishing a 4-year Dynamic Purchasing System (2024-2028) for COVID-19 vaccines to support future immunisation campaigns. The potential total value is £1 billion excluding VAT, with no minimum volume commitment. Suppliers must be approved on the DPS before participating in future tender opportunities.
what_they_want: COVID-19 vaccines and product(s) indicated for protection from COVID-19 for immunisation campaigns. No specific brands or model numbers mentioned.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably qualified suppliers must apply and be accepted onto the Dynamic Purchasing System via the Atamis eTendering system. Only suppliers whose DPS application has been approved at the time an ITT is issued will be eligible to submit tenders. Specific qualifying criteria available through the Atamis system.

original_title: ASC23034 Dynamic Purchasing System for the Provision of Older People Residential and Nursing Care Long Term and Replacement Care Services
english_summary: Worcestershire County Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for older people residential and nursing care services across three lots covering long-term care, short-term replacement care, and emergency short-term replacement care. The DPS will run for four years with options to extend up to eight years total, with an estimated lifetime value of £1,107,679,301 excluding VAT.
what_they_want: Older people residential and nursing care services divided into three lots: Lot 1 - Long Term Care, Lot 2 - Short Term Replacement Care, and Lot 3 - Emergency Short Term Replacement Care.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably experienced, qualified and appropriately accredited suppliers. Applicants must complete a Standard Selection Questionnaire and upload relevant documents.

original_title: Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS)
buyer_name: North Staffordshire Combined Healthcare NHS Trust
english_summary: North Staffordshire Combined Healthcare NHS Trust is reopening their Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) covering North Staffordshire and Stoke on Trent. The DPS, which went live in February 2023 for 5 years, allows qualified organizations to bid on CAMHS service contracts offering face-to-face, digital, and blended delivery approaches for children aged 0-25 years.
what_they_want: Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) for ages 0-25 years (primarily 0-18 years) including multi-disciplinary team approaches, strengths-based and trauma informed care, outcome measurement tools, and services delivered through face-to-face, digital, and blended modalities.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably experienced organisations with expertise in Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services. Applicants must indicate their area(s) of expertise and delivery modalities as part of the qualification process.

original_title: CHC Nursing Home Beds Preferred Provider List- NHS BLMK ICB
buyer_name: NHS Bedfordshire, Luton & Milton Keynes Integrated Care Board
english_summary: NHS Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes Integrated Care Board is seeking qualified providers of Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds to deliver safe and appropriate care in registered nursing home settings across the BLMK population. This is an Any Qualified Provider (AQP) framework for a 5-year period with optional 2-year extension.
what_they_want: Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds services to be delivered in registered nursing home settings for clients assessed as requiring this level of care.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably qualified providers of CHC nursing home beds services with formal registered nursing home facilities capable of delivering services across Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes population.

original_title: CHC Nursing Home Beds Preferred Provider List- NHS Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes ICB
english_summary: NHS Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes Integrated Care Board is seeking qualified providers of Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds to deliver safe and appropriate care in registered nursing home settings across Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes. This is an Any Qualified Provider (AQP) contract on a zero value, zero volume basis for 5 years with an optional 2-year extension.
what_they_want: CHC (Continuing Healthcare) nursing home beds services to be delivered in formal registered nursing home settings for clients assessed as requiring such placements.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably qualified providers of CHC Nursing Homes Beds services. Specific certification and experience requirements are contained in the documentation on the Atamis platform (project C153293).

original_title: Mental Health Recovery
english_summary: Rotherham Metropolitan Borough Council is seeking experienced providers to deliver community mental health care and support services through a Flexible Purchasing System (FPS). The services will provide mental health recovery support and personalized social care to help people with mental ill-health achieve independent living in their communities.
what_they_want: Community mental health care and support services including supported living accommodation-based services and day opportunities, all based on mental health recovery principles. NOT medical devices or equipment.
eligibility_requirements: Experienced providers with capability and capacity to deliver community mental health care and support. Must register with the Social Value Portal and commit to delivering Social Value additionality via all contracts.

original_title: DPS 4 - Supply of community equipment for small moving & handling equipment
english_summary: Croydon Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the supply of community equipment to the Integrated Procurement Hub, a shared service arrangement with multiple local authorities. This specific tender (DPS 4) covers 32 categories of small moving and handling equipment.
what_they_want: Small moving and handling equipment across 32 categories of community equipment. The tender is part of a broader DPS system that also includes complex community equipment (31 categories), paediatric community equipment (28 categories), and simple aids community equipment (102 categories).
eligibility_requirements: Suppliers must meet DPS admission criteria set out in the Pre-Qualification Questionnaire (PQQ) to become an "Admitted Supplier." Unsuccessful suppliers may re-submit revised PQQs at any time. Suppliers can register at any time during the DPS validity period.

notice_id: Croydon Integrated Procurement Hub - DN179859 - DPS3
original_title: DPS 3 - Supply of community equipment for Simple Aids
english_summary: Croydon Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the supply of simple aids community equipment on behalf of itself and partner local authorities through the Integrated Procurement Hub. This is DPS 3, covering 102 categories of simple aids, as part of a larger framework that also includes complex and paediatric equipment categories.
what_they_want: 102 categories of simple aids community equipment (specific device types and models not detailed in notice)
eligibility_requirements: Suppliers must meet DPS admission criteria set out in the Pre-Qualification Questionnaire (PQQ) to become an "Admitted Supplier" and be registered on the DPS for one or more product categories. Unsuccessful suppliers may resubmit revised PQQs at any time.

original_title: DPS 2 - Supply of community equipment paediatric and young adults
english_summary: Croydon Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the supply of paediatric and young adult community equipment on behalf of itself and partner local authorities through the Integrated Procurement Hub. This is DPS_2 covering 28 categories of paediatric community equipment, operating as an open system where suppliers can join at any time during its validity period.
what_they_want: 28 categories of paediatric and young adult community equipment (specific categories and model numbers not detailed in this notice)
eligibility_requirements: Suppliers must meet DPS admission criteria set out in the Pre-Qualification Questionnaire (PQQ). Unsuccessful suppliers may resubmit revised PQQs at any time. No specific certifications, experience levels, or company size requirements mentioned in this notice.

original_title: Provision of Non-Emergency Patient Transport Services (NEPTS) for GHFT
english_summary: Gloucestershire Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust is seeking to procure Non-Emergency Patient Transport Services (NEPTS) to transport patients who do not require emergency care to and from hospital facilities.
what_they_want: Non-Emergency Patient Transport Services (NEPTS) - transport services for patients requiring non-urgent medical transportation to and from healthcare facilities.

original_title: Occupational Health and Related Services - DPS
english_summary: Crown Commercial Service is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for occupational health and related services including occupational health, employee assistance programmes, fully managed services, eyecare and hearing services. This will be available to public sector, wider public sector and third sector organizations in the UK.
what_they_want: Occupational health services, employee assistance programmes, fully managed service, eyecare services, and hearing services.

original_title: Health Checks DPS
english_summary: West Northamptonshire Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for delivery of NHS Health Check services to identify and manage cardiovascular risk factors in people aged 40-74 years. The program aims to offer health checks to 49,500 eligible residents annually, with an aspiration of 34,650 checks completed per year.
what_they_want: NHS Health Check services for cardiovascular disease screening and risk assessment for eligible population aged 40-74 years with no previous history of CVD. The service should deliver health checks to identify and manage cardiovascular risk factors and disease.

original_title: Facilities Management & Workplace Services DPS
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for Facilities Management and Workplace Services aimed at lower value/less complex contracts and SME suppliers. It covers maintenance, repairs, installation works, CAFM, help-desk services, and waste management for UK public sector estates.
what_they_want: Facilities management and workplace services including maintenance, response repairs, supply and installation works, CAFM (Computer-Aided Facilities Management), help-desk services, and waste management services.

original_title: Provision of Best Interest Assessors / Section 12 Doctors - Pseudo Open Framework
english_summary: Hull City Council is seeking to establish a pseudo open framework agreement for the provision of Best Interest Assessors and Section 12 Doctors under the Light Touch Regime. The framework will run for 3 years from April 2025 to March 2028 with an optional 24-month extension, with an estimated value of £200,000 per annum.
what_they_want: Lot 1 - Best Interest Assessors; Lot 2 - Section 12 Doctors

original_title: Functional Electrical Stimulation Supplies and Maintenance
buyer_name: Procurement Assist Limited c/o Integrated Facilities Management Bolton Ltd (iFM Bolton)
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the supply and maintenance of Functional Electrical Stimulation (FES) equipment and supplies to UK public healthcare providers and other public sector organizations. The DPS has been extended to run until 31 May 2029.
what_they_want: Functional Electrical Stimulation (FES) supplies and maintenance services for use in general rehabilitation to complement therapy interventions.

original_title: National Dynamic Purchasing System for Mobile Diagnostic and Treatment Units
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for mobile diagnostic and treatment units to increase NHS capacity for elective procedures and reduce waiting lists. Units can be vans or non-permanent cabins deployed at hospital sites or community locations like supermarkets and GP practices.
what_they_want: Mobile diagnostic and/or treatment units (vans or non-permanent units/cabins) available through three procurement options: Lot 1 - Hire or Lease; Lot 2 - Purchase; Lot 3 - Managed Service.

original_title: P0819A - Application to join the Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the Domestic Homicide Reporting
english_summary: Birmingham City Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) to procure Independent Author/Chair services to conduct Domestic Homicide Reviews across seven West Midlands local authorities. These reviews examine deaths of persons aged 16+ resulting from domestic violence, abuse or neglect to identify lessons and prevent future homicides.
what_they_want: Independent Author/Chair services (combined role) to undertake statutory Domestic Homicide Reviews for deaths resulting from domestic violence, abuse or neglect across the West Midlands region covering Birmingham, Coventry, Dudley, Sandwell, Solihull, Walsall, and Wolverhampton.

original_title: Nursing Homes Preferred Provider List For Derby and Derbyshire ICB
buyer_name: NHS Derby & Derbyshire Integrated Care Board
english_summary: NHS Derby & Derbyshire Integrated Care Board is establishing a Nursing Home Preferred Provider List using an Any Qualified Provider (AQP) basis, similar to a Dynamic Purchasing System. The agreement runs for 5 years from August 1, 2021, with an optional 2-year extension, allowing providers to apply at any time during the contract period.
what_they_want: Nursing home services for Derby and Derbyshire ICB area. This is a zero value, zero volume contract for placement on a preferred provider list.

original_title: Nursing Homes Preferred Provider List For Derby and Derbyshire ICB
english_summary: NHS Derby & Derbyshire ICB is seeking to establish a Nursing Home Preferred Provider List using an Any Qualified Provider (AQP) basis, similar to a Dynamic Purchasing System. Providers can apply at any time during the contract period, which runs for 5 years with an optional 2-year extension.
what_they_want: Nursing home services for placement on a Preferred Provider List.

original_title: Adults with Disabilities Pseudo-DPS
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for providing home and community support services to working-age adults (16+) with disabilities and/or long-term health conditions living in Sheffield, running from March 27, 2023 to March 26, 2033.
what_they_want: Support services for adults with disabilities including in-home support and activities/support outside the home for people aged 16 or over with disabilities and/or long-term health conditions.

original_title: Children and Young People Continuing Healthcare
buyer_name: NHS Arden and Greater East Midlands Commissioning Support Unit
english_summary: This is a tender for continuing healthcare services for children and young people in Norfolk and Waveney who have been determined to have Continuing Care needs. The service must be available 365 days per year, 24 hours a day, and will be awarded as a Preferred Provider List on a zero value, zero volume basis for 3 years.
what_they_want: Continuing healthcare services for children and young people with Continuing Care needs, available 365 days per year, 24 hours per day, delivered in accordance with service specifications and individual care plans.

original_title: Pseudo Dynamic Purchasing System (PDPS) for Rapid Specialist Opinion (RSO) / Referral Management Services (RMS)
buyer_name: NHS NORTH OF ENGLAND COMMISSIONING SUPPORT (HOSTED BY NHS ENGLAND)
english_summary: This tender is for establishing a Pseudo Dynamic Purchasing System (PDPS) for Rapid Specialist Opinion (RSO) and Referral Management Services (RMS) on behalf of multiple NHS Clinical Commissioning Groups in North East England. The system will allow NHS organizations to procure specialist medical opinion and referral management services.
what_they_want: Rapid Specialist Opinion (RSO) services and Referral Management Services (RMS) for healthcare referrals and specialist clinical opinions.

original_title: Flexible Procurement System (FPS) for the Provision of Independent Assessors and Reviewers
english_summary: The City of Doncaster Council is establishing a Flexible Procurement System for independent assessors and reviewers across seven lots covering mental health assessments, safeguarding reviews, and complaints procedures. Providers can apply for one or multiple lots covering areas including Deprivation of Liberties assessments, children's and adult safeguarding reviews, and domestic homicide reviews.
what_they_want: NOT FOUND

original_title: Older Peoples Care Homes
english_summary: This tender is for the provision of care home services for older people in Sheffield, focusing on personalized care that promotes independence, wellbeing, and quality of life while supporting individuals to remain connected to their communities and reducing unnecessary hospital admissions.
what_they_want: NOT FOUND
